title: Effective queue state requires an explicit commit horizon
sources: recent repository commit inventory inspected through 0efd9b4c7f53053b91948faa81bbd43daa1c61f5

memory.observed: The canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE.json predates later team queue-update commits, including the continuity reconciliation update at 47bf4e661a2f8b5ff62b1ccf77d4da265121bf8e and subsequent cross-team work.
memory.inferred: An effective queue projection without a declared base blob and terminal commit horizon is temporally ambiguous: two readers can reconcile different update sets and both appear internally consistent.
memory.proposed: Every reconciled queue projection must declare base_queue_blob_sha, inclusive_terminal_commit, enumerated_update_paths, and excluded_later_commits. Selection may use that projection only within the declared horizon.
memory.superseded: A materialized queue is current merely because its file timestamp is newer than ACTIVE_QUEUE.json.
memory.unresolved: No authoritative projection inspected in this run proves exhaustive enumeration of all queue updates from the canonical base through the latest repository commit.

contradiction_resolution.apparent_conflict: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json presents an older canonical state while later queue-update files present newer active work.
contradiction_resolution.resolution: These are not interchangeable claims. The canonical file is the base authority; later records are additive evidence whose effective authority is bounded by an explicit enumeration horizon.
contradiction_resolution.selection_rule: Prefer a schema-valid projection whose base bytes, update manifest, terminal commit, and output bytes are all bound by receipt. Otherwise report canonical state and additive state separately.

falsification_route: Generate an authenticated exhaustive queue-update manifest from the canonical base through a declared terminal commit, materialize the projection, and verify a receipt binding the base, manifest, horizon, and output bytes.
